mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near zero-lower-bound levels (below 1%) reflects an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon as long as the rate remains low.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX exhibits negative mean 60-day return during periods when FEDFUNDS < 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of at least 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-style threshold) signals an emerging recessionary shift in labor demand, triggering risk-off equity flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signals robust near-term corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither series alone is sufficient because retail strength without housing follow-through often reflects transitory spending rather than sustained demand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 4%
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no incremental interaction value.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its trailing 5-year average during an economic expansion regime indicates healthy capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is expected to be much weaker or absent outside expansion regimes.
regime_hint: expansion regime defined by positive trailing 4-quarter GDPC1 growth
failure_condition: The positive INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically within expansion-regime subsamples over any 3-year rolling window, or holds equally strongly in contraction regimes, invalidating the regime-conditionality claim.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI print that exceeds 0.4% signals an inflation surprise that raises near-term expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, causing an immediate negative repricing of equities over the following trading week as discount rate expectations rise.
regime_hint: any regime with rising inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following triggered high-CPI prints are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects sustained labor market expansion that supports consumer income and spending, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the following month.
regime_hint: any regime with positive payroll growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward SPX return is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative payroll growth periods do not show materially different forward returns than positive growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound, positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals demand recovery without the offsetting drag of monetary tightening, producing strong positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; the same GDP growth in a high-rate regime is expected to produce a muted or negative response due to tightening risk.
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary regime (FEDFUNDS < 1%) with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: The GDPC1-return relationship shows no meaningful difference in sign or magnitude between FEDFUNDS < 1% and FEDFUNDS > 3% regimes, or Rank IC in the low-rate regime falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) within the same reporting month signals a compounding supply-side contraction that markets have not yet fully priced, producing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week; neither weak INDPRO nor rising UNRATE alone reliably signals this acute stress without the other confirming it.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0
regime_hint: early recessionary or sharp slowdown regime
failure_condition: The joint-trigger 5-day forward return is not significantly more negative than either single-series trigger's return (p > 0.10), or triggered instances show positive returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward window.
